summary: coral-wraith postinstall exfiltrates AWS IAM credentials and ~/.npmrc to hardcoded C2
details: On npm install, postinstall.js executes a credential-harvest payload gated on os.hostname() matching /^[0-9a-f]{12}$/ (the Docker container-ID shape), so the script lies dormant on developer laptops and fires only inside containerized/CI targets. When the gate matches, it cats /root/.npmrc and /home/node/.npmrc, queries the AWS Instance Metadata Service at 169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/ for IAM role credentials, runs `aws sts get-caller-identity` and `aws secretsmanager list-secrets` / `get-secret-value`, and also searches for an HTB{...} flag string. The harvested output is chunked into ~240-byte parts and PUT over plain HTTP to a hardcoded bare-IP C2 at http://154.57.164.71:30782 (with a 127.0.0.1:30782 local fallback). The package's index.js exports {} and provides no advertised functionality — the install-time exfil payload is the package's only purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
